steps.title: Express the scaling relationship
steps.content: If triangle S has a side of length $s$, and we multiply by $k$ to get triangle T, then triangle T's side is $k \cdot s$. For the new side to be larger, we need $k \cdot s > s$, which simplifies to $k > 1$.

steps.title: Test the choices
steps.content:
We need $k > 1$:
  • A) $\frac{29}{28} \approx 1.036 > 1$ ✓
  • B) \$1 is not greater than \$1
  • C) $\frac{28}{29} \approx 0.966 < 1$
  • D) $0 < 1$
That matches choice A — A is correct.
